# RCX-Registry

> An MCP-compatible subregistry serving a mirror of more than 17,000 servers from the official Model Context Protocol registry. The repository publishes a frozen Ed25519 receipt format and byte-exact vectors. Hosted production currently has zero snapshots because Vault Transit returns 403, and every public publisher verification/declaration write returns 404. Spec v1 defines type-specific snapshot and enrichment links, not one universal receipt chain.

## What it is

- [Home](https://rcxprotocol.org/): what RCX-Registry is and why tamper-evident history matters (led by real 2025–26 MCP supply-chain incidents).
- [Verify](https://rcxprotocol.org/verify): canonical encodings, the flat BLAKE3 snapshot-set digest, receipt signatures, exact link semantics, conformance checks, and current public-verification limits.
- [Protocol Spec v1](https://rcxprotocol.org/spec/v1): the normative, versioned wire-format freeze with canonical CBOR/JSON, hashing, receipts, snapshot chaining, API/error rules, and downloadable conformance vectors.
- Independent, open-source (Apache-2.0). Not affiliated with Anthropic or the MCP project.

## Protocol specification

- `rcx-spec/v1` is published from source commit `310ace858172b6f4acdd982ef25c2441f20d6b7a`.
- [Normative chapters](https://rcxprotocol.org/spec/v1) — the complete specification and chapter navigation.
- [Conformance vectors](https://rcxprotocol.org/spec/v1/vectors) — exact JSON fixtures; individual vector URLs return the repository bytes unchanged.

## API endpoints (registry.rcxprotocol.org)

- `GET /v0/servers` — list servers (cursor pagination, `limit`); shape-compatible with the upstream MCP registry.
- `GET /v0/servers/{name}/versions` — list versions for a server.
- `GET /v0/servers/{name}/versions/{version}` — fetch one version.
- `GET https://rcxprotocol.org/openapi.json` — self-contained OpenAPI spec describing the registry API.

## Publisher verification

- [Publish](https://rcxprotocol.org/publish): current fail-closed publisher status and the gates required before DNS or GitHub proof can reopen.
- DNS challenge/verify, GitHub start/callback, manual review, and `POST /v0/publishers/declare` all return 404 at the production edge.
- [Badge](https://rcxprotocol.org/badge): README badge guidance and the distinction between a visual label and authoritative API verification metadata.

## Subregistry positioning

- [Subregistry](https://rcxprotocol.org/subregistry): RCX-Registry as a spec-compliant MCP subregistry — shape compatibility, the `_meta.org.rcxprotocol.registry` contract, client configuration, and the currently degraded sync/signing state.
